semanticCore: Distraction, play, amusement, diversion, or neglect
quranicRelevance: Distraction from truth/remembrance (Lahw - 'alhakumu at-takathur'), amusement/vanity ('lahwon wa la'ibon'), and distracted hearts (Lahiyah).

keyDerivations.meaning: Amusement, diversion, vanity, distraction, or play
To distract, divert, keep busy, or make heedless

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَوْ أَرَدْنَا أَنْ نَتَّخِذَ لَهْوًا (21:17): If We had willed to take a play/pastime (refuting divine vanity).
لَاهِيَةً قُلُوبُهُمْ: Distracted/heedless are their hearts.
discardedNoise: Obscure details of throwing meat to dogs.
summaryEn: The root represents distraction and amusement (Lahw), depicting the heedless diversion from eternal goals by social competition and material toys.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التلهي والتشاغل، واصفاً اللهو الصارف عن الغايات الكبرى (لهو) والتكاثر الملهي للقلوب الغافلة (ألهاكم).
rawText: أَلْهَانِى الشَّىْءُ 4 The thing diverted me; syn. شَغَلَنِى. (Msb.) === أَلْهَاهُ بَالغِنَآءِ [He diverted him by singing]. (S, art. سمد.) تَلَهَّى بَالشَّىْءِ 5 i. q. تَعَلَّلَ: (TA:) and أُولِعَ بِهِ. (Msb.) === And تَلِّهَى He diverted himself. (TA.) لَهَاةٌ [The uvula;] the red piece of flesh that hangs down from the upper حَنَكِ. (Zj, in his Khalk el-Insán.) See also الأَسَالِقُ, and شِقْشِقةٌ. === اللَهُاة [generally expl. as meaning The uvula: or] what is between the end of the root of the tongue and the end of the قَلْب [thus in all the copies of the K that I have seen, an evident mistranscription for قَلْت, i. e. hollow] of the upper part of the mouth (K: [app. meaning the arches, or pillars, of the soft palate; agreeably with the next explanation here following:]) or the furthest part of the mouth: [see غُنّةٌ:] and, of the he-camel, the شِقْشِقَة [i. e. bursa faucium]. (JK.) See also a usage of the pl., لَهَواتٌ, in the last explanation of قَلْتٌ. لَهِىَ عَنْهُ 1 , (S, Msb, K) and لَهَا عنه (Msb, K,) He became diverted from it, so as to forget it. (S, Msb, K.) === See an ex. voce استأثر: and see عَنْ. === لَهَتْ إِلَى حَدِيثِهِ, inf. n. لَهْوٌ and لُهُوٌّ, She (a woman) was, or became, cheered, or delighted, and pleased, with his discourse. (M, K.) And so لَهَوْتُ بِحَدِيثِهِ. (T in art. رنو.) لَهْوٌ Diversion; pastime; sport; play: or especially, such as is vain, or frivolous; idle sport: (from various explanations:) what occupies a man so as to divert him from that which would render him sad or solicitous, &c.: (TA:) or relief of the mind by means which wisdom does not require: this [it is said] is the original signification: (Et-Tarasoosee, Msb:) a thing in which a man delights himself, and which occupies him so as to divert him, and then ceases. (KT.) It has a more general application than لَعِبٌ: for ex., the hearing of musical instruments or the like is لَهْوٌ, but not لَعْبٌ. (TA.) === آلَةُ لَهْوٍ [An instrument of diversion, meaning, of music]. (K voce رَبَابٌ.) لُهْوَةٌ What is thrown, [i. e. the quantity of corn that is thrown,] (S, K,) by the grinder, with his hand, (S,) into the mouth of the mill or millstone. (S, K.) And The mouth [itself] of the mill or mill-stone. (IKtt, TA; and S voce اخُرٌّ.)
